skill.name: scikit-learn
skill.description: Guides Python machine learning work with scikit-learn, including supervised learning, unsupervised learning, preprocessing, evaluation, tuning, and production-style pipelines.
skill.summary: Build, evaluate, and tune classical machine learning workflows with scikit-learn.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, script, network, and keyword findings, but review found they are mostly markdown code fences, Python imports, ML terminology, and documentation links. No prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or malicious intent was found. The main publishable concern is that examples load pickle or joblib model files, which can execute code if users load untrusted artifacts.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe Model Deserialization Pattern
medium_findings.description: The skill shows loading models from pickle and joblib files. This is a legitimate ML workflow, but pickle-based formats can execute code when the file is untrusted, so users need a clear trust-boundary warning.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly demonstrate deserializing local model files. The examples are not malicious, but pickle and joblib loading are known unsafe when artifacts are not trusted.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command findings are fenced installation or run examples in documentation. They do not execute during skill loading and do not interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown code fences containing package installation or script invocation examples. There is no runtime command execution path in the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Python Imports Misclassified as Dynamic Code Loading
low_findings.description: The dynamic import findings are ordinary Python import statements for scikit-learn, NumPy, pandas, matplotlib, and metrics modules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code imports named library modules and does not use eval, exec, importlib, or user-controlled module names. The semantic context is standard ML example code.

low_findings.title: Security Keyword Heuristics Triggered by ML Terminology
low_findings.description: C2 and weak-crypto alerts appear to come from normal machine learning terms such as PC2, DBSCAN noise labels, SGD, and regression wording. No command-and-control behavior or cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited terms are embedded in PCA, clustering, and stochastic-gradient-descent examples. Their usage is domain-specific ML language, not security tooling or malicious infrastructure.

low_findings.title: External Links and Vendor Suggestion Require Disclosure
low_findings.description: Network findings are documentation URLs and one suggestion to use K-Dense Web for complex workflows. They do not make network calls, but marketplace users should see this as external-service guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is visible documentation text, not executable network code. The vendor recommendation is benign but should be transparent in marketplace review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pickle-Based Model Loading
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples use pickle or joblib loading for saved models. This pattern should only be used with trusted files because deserialization can execute code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code demonstrates direct deserialization of model files. The risk depends on artifact trust, so this is a medium-confidence publish warning rather than a block.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Output in Example Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: Example scripts save plot images to the current working directory. This is expected behavior for analysis scripts, but users should run examples in a workspace they control.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly save PNG outputs. The filenames are hardcoded and non-sensitive, so the impact is limited to expected local file creation.

content.value_statement: Classical machine learning work often fails because preprocessing, evaluation, and tuning are handled inconsistently. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured scikit-learn guidance for reliable pipelines and model comparison.
actual_capabilities: Explains supervised learning choices for classification and regression models.
Guides preprocessing with scalers, imputers, encoders, and feature engineering tools.
Shows how to compose Pipeline and ColumnTransformer workflows.
Covers model evaluation metrics, cross-validation, and hyperparameter search.
Includes example scripts for classification pipelines and clustering analysis.
Provides quick references for algorithm selection and common scikit-learn patterns.
limitations: Does not train models unless the user provides data and asks to run code.
Does not replace domain validation, data quality review, or fairness assessment.
Examples assume Python, scikit-learn, NumPy, pandas, and related packages are installed.
Serialized model loading examples should only use trusted model files.

use_cases.title: Create a Baseline Classifier
use_cases.description: Build a clean train-test split, preprocessing flow, model fit, and metric report for tabular classification.

use_cases.title: Compare Candidate Models
use_cases.description: Use cross-validation and consistent metrics to compare algorithms before deeper tuning.

use_cases.title: Prepare Teaching Examples
use_cases.description: Explain common scikit-learn workflows with concise examples for learners and project teams.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Model Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a first scikit-learn workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a scikit-learn workflow for my dataset. I have columns for features, one target column, and a classification goal. Ask for any missing details before suggesting models.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs preprocessing and model training together.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a scikit-learn Pipeline for numeric and categorical features. Include imputation, scaling, one-hot encoding, a classifier, and clear evaluation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate and Tune
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user wants reliable model comparison.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my scikit-learn model evaluation approach. Recommend cross-validation, metrics, and hyperparameter search settings for an imbalanced classification problem.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an ML Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user wants a rigorous review before production use.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this scikit-learn workflow for data leakage, unstable preprocessing, weak metrics, unsafe model loading, and deployment risks. Suggest concrete fixes in priority order.

output_examples.input: I need a churn classifier with numeric and categorical columns.
output: A recommended pipeline structure with separate numeric and categorical preprocessing.
A short list of baseline classifiers to compare first.
Evaluation guidance using stratified splitting and classification metrics.
A checklist for scaling, dimensionality reduction, and cluster count selection.
Suggested metrics such as silhouette and Davies-Bouldin score.
Guidance on visual inspection and sensitivity testing.
A trust-boundary warning for pickle and joblib artifacts.
Recommendations for versioning dependencies and validating loaded models.
A reminder to avoid loading model files from unknown sources.

best_practices: Use Pipeline or ColumnTransformer so preprocessing is fit only on training data.
Choose metrics that match the business goal, class balance, and prediction cost.
Set random_state values and document data splits for reproducible experiments.
anti_patterns: Fitting scalers, imputers, or encoders on the full dataset before splitting.
Choosing a model only from test-set performance after repeated manual tuning.
Loading pickle or joblib model files from untrusted sources.

faq.question: What tasks does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers scikit-learn classification, regression, clustering, preprocessing, pipelines, evaluation, and tuning workflows.

faq.question: Can it work with my private dataset?
faq.answer: Yes, if you provide the relevant schema, sample rows, or local file context to your AI tool.

faq.question: Does it run model training automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Code only runs when your environment executes the generated commands or scripts.

faq.question: Is this only for tabular data?
faq.answer: No. It also discusses text features, clustering, dimensionality reduction, and general scikit-learn utilities.

faq.question: How should I avoid data leakage?
faq.answer: Split data before fitting preprocessing steps, or use Pipeline so transformations are learned inside cross-validation.

faq.question: Are saved model files safe to load?
faq.answer: Only load pickle or joblib model files from trusted sources because deserialization can execute code.
